# Audit checklist

This document tracks every static-analysis and invariant-test finding raised against the Helios Solidity surface, and how each finding has been resolved (mitigated, accepted as known limitation, or queued for fix). It is the human-side gate for the Phase 6 security pass — `.github/workflows/security.yml` is informational only and never blocks a merge.

**Scope.** All Solidity in `contracts/src/` plus the autogenerated snarkjs Groth16 verifiers in `contracts/src/verifiers/`. Third-party libraries (`contracts/lib/`), tests, deploy scripts, and mocks are filtered out of automated scans (see `contracts/slither.config.json`).

**Audience.** Helios maintainers running pre-release security passes, plus external auditors performing a one-shot review. Each row is meant to give a reviewer enough context to decide whether the triage decision was reasonable without re-reading the contract.

**Threat-model framework.** Severity assignments tie back to the trust assumptions and threat scenarios in `Helios.md §15`. Where a finding maps cleanly onto a documented threat, the **Mitigation** column references the relevant subsection.

**Status legend.**

- **Accepted** — known limitation, behaviour is intentional, no code change required. Reviewer rationale recorded inline.
- **Mitigated** — code-level defence already prevents the failure mode (gating modifier, reentrancy guard, validation, etc.).
- **Fixed** — finding addressed by a specific commit / PR (linked in Mitigation).
- **Open** — accepted onto the backlog; not blocking v1.
- **False positive** — detector misread; explanation inline.

---

## Slither

Last run: 2026-05-08, against commit at HEAD of `main` (Phase 5 cross-chain shipped). Run reproduced locally with `slither-analyzer 0.11.5` and `solc 0.8.28`. CI re-runs on every pull request via `.github/workflows/security.yml`.

Aggregate counts: **9 High, 27 Medium, 57 Low, 227 Informational** (320 findings total). The High and Medium tables below are exhaustive (deduplicated to one row per detector × call-site cluster). Low and Informational findings are not enumerated here — they are dominated by `naming-convention` (195 of them, almost all in the autogenerated verifiers) and `timestamp` lint on the auto-defund freshness checks; both classes are reviewed in bulk and rolled forward unless a specific entry is escalated.

### High severity

All 9 High findings are `incorrect-return` in the snarkjs-generated Groth16 verifiers. These are well-known false positives in `snarkjs@0.7.6`'s template: the assembly blocks intentionally `return(0, 0x20)` on field-element validation failure, which Slither cannot distinguish from an unexpected halt. The verifier outputs are fixed for any given circuit and are validated end-to-end by circuit unit tests (`circuits/Makefile` → `make test`) plus the `TradeAttestationVerifier` integration tests. Replacing them is a snarkjs version-pin migration, not a code-fix (`MEMORY.md → snarkjs pinned to exact 0.7.6`).

| ID | Contract | Finding | Severity | Status | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SL-H-01 | `verifiers/MomentumV1Verifier.sol` | `incorrect-return` in `verifyProof` (`asm_0.checkField`, `checkPairing`, `g1_mulAccC`) — 3 sites | High | False positive | Autogenerated by snarkjs 0.7.6. Halts (`return(0, 0x20)`) are intentional field-validation failures. End-to-end correctness is covered by `circuits/test` proof-generation tests + on-chain integration tests in `contracts/test/TradeAttestationVerifier.t.sol`. |
| SL-H-02 | `verifiers/MeanReversionV1Verifier.sol` | `incorrect-return` in `verifyProof` (same 3 sites) | High | False positive | Same template + same mitigation as SL-H-01. |
| SL-H-03 | `verifiers/YieldRotationV1Verifier.sol` | `incorrect-return` in `verifyProof` (same 3 sites) | High | False positive | Same template + same mitigation as SL-H-01. |

### Medium severity

| ID | Contract | Finding | Severity | Status | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SL-M-01 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `allocateToStrategy` — state writes (`rec.strategy`, `rec.capitalDeployed`, `_userTotalDeployed`) follow external calls into `IUserVaultForAllocator.transferToAllocator` and `IStrategyVault.allocateFrom`. | Medium | Mitigated | Function carries `nonReentrant` (line 232, ReentrancyGuardTransient). External callees are vault contracts owned by Helios and gated by `onlyAllocatorVault` modifiers; they cannot call back into `AllocatorVault`. Slither does not model the gating. |
| SL-M-02 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `_allocateInternal` (used by `allocateToStrategy` and `rebalance`) | Medium | Mitigated | Same as SL-M-01. Both public entry points are `nonReentrant`. |
| SL-M-03 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `_withdrawPartial` | Medium | Mitigated | Internal helper called only from `rebalance` (`nonReentrant`, line 467) and `defundStrategy` (`nonReentrant`, line 288). |
| SL-M-04 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `_unwindAndCredit` | Medium | Mitigated | Internal helper called only from `defundStrategy`, `finalizeDefund`, both `nonReentrant`. |
| SL-M-05 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `defundStrategy` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 288). External callees are Helios-controlled vaults. |
| SL-M-06 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `finalizeDefund` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 388). |
| SL-M-07 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `rebalance` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 467). |
| SL-M-08 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `settleStrategyFee` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 524). |
| SL-M-09 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `settleStrategyFee` (`realizedTotal == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | Strict equality on `uint256` returned by `distributeRealized` is intentional — a return of exactly zero means "nothing realized this epoch", and the function early-returns to skip HWM update. Cannot underflow because `realizedTotal` is a fresh return value, not an accumulator. |
| SL-M-10 | `OraclePriceAnchor.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `latest()` (`n == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | `n` is the post-incremented commit counter; a zero check is the canonical "no commits yet" sentinel and matches the contract's invariant that commit IDs start at 1. |
| SL-M-11 | `OracleYieldAnchor.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `latest()` (`n == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | Same pattern as SL-M-10. |
| SL-M-12 | `StrategyRegistry.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `initiateParamsRotation` (`_paramsHashOf[strategyId] == bytes32(0)`) | Medium | Accepted | Used to gate "strategy must already be registered before its params can rotate." Zero is the deliberate unregistered sentinel. |
| SL-M-13 | `StrategyRegistry.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `completeParamsRotation` (`p.newHash == bytes32(0)`) | Medium | Accepted | Same pattern; zero indicates "no rotation pending." |
| SL-M-14 | `StrategyVault.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `_checkNavDivergence` (`markedFloor == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | Intentional early-return when the cash-floor check has no floor to compare against (zero deployed). Maps to the §6.4 cash-floor invariant in `Helios.md`. |
| SL-M-15 | `UserVault.sol` | `uninitialized-local` — `found` flag in `setMetaStrategy`, `_isAddressSubset`, `_isBytes32Subset`, `_isUint32Subset` (4 sites) | Medium | Accepted | Solidity zero-initialises stack variables; the boolean flags rely on the default `false`. Idiomatic pattern for subset-check helpers. Slither warns by category; behaviour is defined. |
| SL-M-16 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `uninitialized-local` — `reopening` (in `allocateToStrategy` and `_allocateInternal`), `sum`, `totalDeployed` (in `rebalance`) | Medium | Accepted | Same as SL-M-15: integers and bools default to zero / false. |
| SL-M-17 | `AllocatorRegistry.sol` | `uninitialized-local` — loop counter `j` in `_nameKey` | Medium | Accepted | Default-zero loop counter; idiomatic. |
| SL-M-18 | `StrategyVault.sol` | `uninitialized-local` — `data` in `_forwardAttestationIfRemote` | Medium | Accepted | `bytes memory` initialises empty by default, which is the intended state when no remote forward is needed. |
| SL-M-19 | `verifiers/MomentumV1VerifierAdapter.sol`, `MeanReversionV1VerifierAdapter.sol`, `YieldRotationV1VerifierAdapter.sol` | `uninitialized-local` — `fixedInputs` in `verifyProof` (3 sites) | Medium | Accepted | Adapter copies `pubInputs` into a fixed-size memory array; the array is fully written before being passed to the underlying verifier. False positive in Slither's flow analysis. |

### Low / Informational

The 57 Low and 227 Informational findings break down as:

- 195 `naming-convention` — all in the snarkjs-generated verifiers (`asm_0`, `g1_mulAccC`, etc.). Cannot be renamed without diverging from the generator output.
- 23 `timestamp` — `block.timestamp` reads on auto-defund freshness, oracle staleness, fee crystallisation. Threshold values (180s freshness, 5% defund) make sub-second drift irrelevant. See `Helios.md §6.10`, §6.4.
- 12 `assembly` — verifier templates, `MetaStrategyLib` Poseidon helper. Reviewed during Phase 2 circuit/contract integration.
- 10 `calls-loop` — controlled bounded loops over user-allocations and registered strategies. Bound enforced by `MAX_STRATEGIES` constant in `AllocatorVault`.
- 7 `unused-state` — kept-for-upgradeability storage gaps in UUPS proxies. Intentional.
- 7 `missing-zero-check` — owner-set addresses (oracle anchor, fee receiver). Owner is the Helios multi-sig; treated as trusted (`Helios.md §15.1`).
- 7 `reentrancy-benign` / 7 `reentrancy-events` — same `nonReentrant`-gated paths as SL-M-01..08, just lower-severity event-ordering variants.
- 5 `missing-inheritance` — interfaces declared inline rather than extending. Stylistic, not a defect.
- 4 `cyclomatic-complexity` — `AllocatorVault.rebalance`, `StrategyVault._postTrade`. Acknowledged tradeoff for keeping the fee/HWM logic in one transaction.
- 3 `events-maths` — accumulator-only updates that don't emit (e.g. internal HWM tracking). Subgraph reads from the parent event; no consumer needs the per-step.
- 2 `unindexed-event-address` — minor; will fix in next ABI bump.
- 1 `solc-version` — pragma `^0.8.28` is the current Foundry pin; not changing during Phase 6.
- 1 `low-level-calls` — LayerZero OApp callback invocation; required by the OApp spec.

If any of these escalates during external audit, promote it to its own row in the table above with a status update.

---

## Mythril

Mythril is **not** wired into CI for the reasons documented in `.github/workflows/security.yml` (slow on `via_ir` builds, high overlap with Slither's reentrancy detector, runs frequently OOM on GitHub-hosted runners). Instead, Helios maintainers run it manually against the three vault contracts before each release tag.

Procedure (run from `contracts/`):

```bash
pip install mythril==0.24.8
forge build
myth analyze src/AllocatorVault.sol --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12
myth analyze src/UserVault.sol      --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12
myth analyze src/StrategyVault.sol  --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12
```

Last run: **TODO — populate after first manual run before the v0.5.0 / v1.0.0 release tag.** Until then, Mythril coverage is provided by the qualitative threat-model walk-through in `Helios.md §15.2`.

| ID | Contract | Finding | Severity | Status | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| _(none recorded yet)_ | | | | | |

## Foundry invariants

Property tests live in `contracts/test/invariant/` and `contracts/test/*Invariant.t.sol`. They run on every PR via the `contracts` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`forge test -vv`) and gate merge through the 85% line-coverage check (raised to 90% before submission, per `CLAUDE.md`).

Captured invariants:

| ID | Contract | Invariant | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| INV-01 | `UserVault` | `sum(allocations[user]) ≤ deposits[user]` for every user, every block | Holds | Enforced by `_checkAllocationCap` on every `allocateToStrategy` call; tested via stateful invariant fuzz in `contracts/test/UserVault.t.sol`. |
| INV-02 | `AllocatorVault` | `_userTotalDeployed[user] == Σ allocations[user][s].capitalDeployed` | Holds | Enforced after every allocate/withdraw/defund by paired bookkeeping; ghost variable in invariant test cross-checks every step. |
| INV-03 | `AllocatorVault` | Once `defundedAt > 0`, `capitalDeployed == 0` until the slot is reopened by a fresh `allocateToStrategy` | Holds | `_unwindAndCredit` zeros `capitalDeployed` and `_allocateInternal` resets `defundedAt` when `reopening` is true. |
| INV-04 | `StrategyVault` | `cashHeld + outstandingPositions ≥ markedNAV × (1 - NAV_DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD_BPS/10_000)` (§6.4 cash-floor invariant) | Holds | Enforced by `_checkNavDivergence` on every `reportNAV`; second consecutive breach emits `NavDivergenceObserved`. |
| INV-05 | `TradeAttestationVerifier` | A trade attestation only verifies if `(declaredClass, paramsHash, oracleRoot)` triple matches a `(strategyId, registeredClass)` row in `StrategyRegistry` | Holds | Enforced by `_validateAttestation` in `verifyAndAttest`; tested in `contracts/test/TradeAttestationVerifier.t.sol`. |
| INV-06 | `OraclePriceAnchor` / `OracleYieldAnchor` | `freshness(root)` reverts after 180s; `revokeRoot` makes a previously-valid root revert immediately | Holds | Phase-3 redeploy added `freshness()` and `unrevokeRoot()` (HIGH #6/#9); see `CLAUDE.md → Key addresses` for the redeployed pin. |
| INV-07 | `ReputationAnchor` (V1, registry-bound) | Only the registered signer can post an update; signature is bound to `(strategyId, epoch, score, componentsHash)` | Holds | EIP-712 signature verification on every post; signer registry change is multi-sig. |

Add new invariants here as Phase 6 polish surfaces them. Each row should link the invariant to either a §6 contract clause or a §15.2 threat-model entry in `Helios.md`.

---

## Maintenance

- The Slither table is regenerated by re-running `slither contracts/ --filter-paths "lib/|test/|script/|mocks/"` from the repo root. The CI workflow uploads the same report on every PR; the table below it should track diffs, not the raw output.
- Promote a Low/Informational finding to its own row only when a reviewer is asked to look at it specifically — otherwise the rolled-up summary above is the canonical record.
- Mythril rows land in the table immediately after a manual run; record the symbolic execution timeout used and the contract address (mainnet / testnet).
- Foundry invariant rows are append-only — once an invariant holds and is in CI, it stays in this document even if the corresponding code is refactored, so regressions in future releases are easy to attribute.
